name: Whole Wheat Pita Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon honey (needed to activate 4 teaspoons yeast)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-size bowl, add yeast and honey to warm water. Let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the warm water mixture, all-purpose wheat flour, whole wheat flour, and salt.
3. Pour the yeast mixture into the center of the dry ingredients and stir until a smooth dough forms that can be gathered into a ball.
4. Place the dough in a large, lightly oiled bowl. Cover with a damp towel. Let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled, about 1 to 2 hours.
5. Punch down the dough. Turn it out onto a lightly floured surface.
6. Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces. Shape each piece into a round and place on nonstick cookie sheets.
7. Cover with a damp towel for 30 minutes.
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll each dough piece into a circle about 5 inches in diameter.
9. Place on the cookie sheets. Let stand for another 30 minutes.
10. Preheat oven to 500°F (260°C). Bake on the middle rack for 5 minutes.
11. Remove pita from the cookie sheets and let cool on a wire rack.
12. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator. To serve, reheat wrapped in aluminum foil at 350°F (175°C) for 10 minutes.
Food Exchanges per serving: 1 1/2 STARCH EXCHANGES
CHO: 21g; PRO: 3g; FAT: 0g; CAL: 99
Low-sodium diets: Omit salt.
